title: The Natural History Of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: The Natural History Of Evangelion             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Fri Jul 17, 2020 1:03 pm All right, first, a little bit of background. Cevdet Mehmet Kosemen is a Turkish science-fiction writer whose works frequently deal with themes of biology, evolution, and humanity's place in nature. He is also fairly well known, at least in internet circles, for his highly unconventional alien designs, many of which are from his planet-building project called Snaiad. Recently, he made a long video dealing with the natural, mythical, and man-made inspirations behind the Angels in Neon Genesis Evangelion . It's well worth watching for anyone who's a fan of the series, provided you have an hour and a half to spare. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Vouz5p8H9oI

[]: Re: The Natural History Of Evangelion             Quote Post by Blockio » Sat Jul 18, 2020 6:39 pm Right now I don't have the time to sit down and watch that blame uni for that , but I'll definitely bookmark it

[]: Re: The Natural History Of Evangelion             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Tue Jul 28, 2020 9:58 am You should. It's really cool!

[]: The Natural History Of Evangelion             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Fri Jul 17, 2020 1:03 pm All right, first, a little bit of background. Cevdet Mehmet Kosemen is a Turkish science-fiction writer whose works frequently deal with themes of biology, evolution, and humanity's place in nature. He is also fairly well known, at least in internet circles, for his highly unconventional alien designs, many of which are from his planet-building project called Snaiad. Recently, he made a long video dealing with the natural, mythical, and man-made inspirations behind the Angels in Neon Genesis Evangelion . It's well worth watching for anyone who's a fan of the series, provided you have an hour and a half to spare. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Vouz5p8H9oI
